/cwe/weakness/587.Name: Assignment of a Fixed Address to a Pointer
/cwe/weakness/587.Description: The product sets a pointer to a specific address other than NULL or 0.
/cwe/weakness/587.ExtendedDescription: Using a fixed address is not portable, because that address will probably not be valid in all environments or platforms.

CommonConsequences.Note: If one executes code at a known location, an attacker might be able to inject code there beforehand.
If the code is ported to another platform or environment, the pointer is likely to be invalid and cause a crash.
The data at a known pointer location can be easily read or influenced by an attacker.

Entries.IntroText: This code assumes a particular function will always be found at a particular address. It assigns a pointer to that address and calls the function.

Entries.BodyText: The same function may not always be found at the same memory address. This could lead to a crash, or an attacker may alter the memory at the expected address, leading to arbitrary code execution.

PotentialMitigations.Description: Never set a pointer to a fixed address.
